207.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer correctly records an error, but it does not advance the current position pointer `curPos` after detecting the invalid single symbol. This causes the lexer to get stuck in an infinite loop because `curPos` is not incremented for the erroneous character, leading to repeated processing of the same character.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer correctly records an error, but it does not advance the current position pointer `curPos` after detecting the invalid single symbol. This causes the lexer to get stuck in an infinite loop because `curPos` is not incremented for the erroneous character, leading to repeated processing of the same character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer correctly records an error, but it does not advance the current position `curPos` after consuming the initial character. This causes the next call to `next()` to reprocess the same character, potentially leading to infinite loops or incorrect error reporting. The `curPos` should be advanced even when an error is detected.]
